content.problemStatement:
Stack has a positive integer P(P>1)P(P > 1)P(P>1).

He starts a game where his initial score is 000.

In the ithi^{th}ith turn, with a probability of 12\frac{1}{2}21‚Äã, Stack adds 1Pi‚àí1\frac{1}{P^{i-1}}Pi‚àí11‚Äã to his score.

Stack stops after NNN turns.

Let SiS_iSi‚Äã be his score after iii turns and F(i)F(i)F(i) denote the expected value of Si2S_i^2Si2‚Äã.

For each integer i(1‚â§i‚â§N)i(1 \leq i \leq N)i(1‚â§i‚â§N), find the value of F(i)F(i)F(i) modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format
The only line of input contains two space-separated positive integers NNN and PPP, as mentioned in the statement.



Output Format
Print NNN space-separated integers. For each integer i(1‚â§i‚â§N)i(1 \leq i \leq N)i(1‚â§i‚â§N), output the value of F(i)F(i)F(i) modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Formally, let M=998244353M=998244353M=998244353. It can be shown that the answer can be expressed as an irreducible fraction xy\frac{x}{y}yx‚Äã, where xxx and yyy are integers and y‚â°Ã∏0(modM)y \not \equiv 0 \pmod{M}yÓÄÝ‚â°0(modM).

Output the integer equal to x‚ãÖy‚àí1‚Äämod‚ÄäMx \cdot y^{-1} \bmod M x‚ãÖy‚àí1modM.

In other words, output such an integer zzz that 0‚â§z<M0 \leq z < M0‚â§z<M and z‚ãÖy‚â°x(modM)z \cdot y \equiv x \pmod{M}z‚ãÖy‚â°x(modM).
